title: Variables and Data Types — Day 02
description: Master Python variables, data types, and type conversions — the building blocks of every Python program.

exercises.test: Create a variable called name and assign your name to it. Then print it.
exercises.concept: Variable assignment — creating named containers for data

exercises.test: Create variables for name (string), age (int), gpa (float), and is_active (bool). Print each with its type.
exercises.solution:
name = 'Alice'
age = 20
gpa = 3.75
is_active = True
for v in [name, age, gpa, is_active]:
    print(type(v))
exercises.concept: Four core data types: str, int, float, bool

exercises.test: Convert a string '123' to integer, add 50, then convert back to string and print.
Create two integer variables and compare them using >, ==, and !=. Print each result and its type.
exercises.solution:
a = 10
b = 20
print(a > b, type(a > b))
print(a == b, type(a == b))
print(a != b, type(a != b))
exercises.concept: Boolean comparison operators return bool values

exercises.test: Create a BMI calculator: read weight and height, calculate BMI = weight / height**2, print result.
exercises.solution:
weight = float(input('Weight: '))
height = float(input('Height: '))
bmi = weight / (height ** 2)
print('BMI:', round(bmi, 2))
exercises.concept: Practical application combining input, float, exponents, and round()
